+  Tab switch history behavior: REPLACE, not PUSH.
+    Tab switches use history.replaceState() — they do NOT create history entries.
+    Only drill-down navigations (Home→Detail, Search→Detail) use history.pushState().
+    This prevents the "history trap": Home → Detail → Search (replace) → Back returns to Detail → Back returns to Home.

+Timeout implementation (browser-floor safe):
+  DO NOT use AbortController.timeout() — unavailable on Safari 15 / Chrome 89.
+  Manual timeout pattern used in every fetch function:
+
+    async function fetchWithTimeout(url, options = {}, timeoutMs = 8000) {
+      const controller = new AbortController();
+      const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
+      try {
+        const response = await fetch(url, { ...options, signal: controller.signal });
+        return response;
+      } finally {
+        clearTimeout(timeoutId);
+      }
+    }
+
+  If the caller passes its own signal (for cancellation), link it:
+    caller's signal.addEventListener('abort', () => controller.abort());
+  This ensures both timeout AND caller-initiated cancellation work.
+

objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-token. The [data-theme='light'] block is cut off at `--skeleton-base: #e4e4e7; /* surface` — skeleton-shine, status colors for light mode, shadow overrides for light mode (dark rgba values will look absurdly heavy on white backgrounds), and the closing brace are all missing. This means the delivered token system is literally uncompilable. Shipping this means every light-mode surface has dark-mode shadows (0,0,0,0.3-0.5 opacity on white — ugly black halos), status colors designed for dark backgrounds render on light without adjustment (emerald-400/amber-400 have poor contrast on white), and skeleton shimmer is broken.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the [data-theme='light'] block with: --skeleton-shine, all --status-* overrides for light (e.g., emerald-600, red-600, amber-600, blue-600 for AA compliance on white), --shadow-sm/md/lg/sheet/focus with reduced opacity (rgba(0,0,0,0.06) through rgba(0,0,0,0.12)), --border-accent override, and the closing brace. Every token that references a color must have a light-mode counterpart or an explicit comment stating it passes contrast checks in both modes.

objections.description: The spec says `#/search?q={encodeURIComponent(preset.query)}` but hash-based routing means the query string is INSIDE the hash fragment. The browser's `location.search` will be empty — `location.hash` will contain everything. The navigation.jsx parser must split `location.hash` on `?` to extract query params. This is not documented anywhere. If someone implements useRoute() by reading `new URL(location).searchParams`, every preset-initiated search will silently fail with an empty query. This is a guaranteed bug.
objections.suggested_fix: In Section 3 under routing, add an explicit implementation note: 'Hash query params are NOT accessible via location.search. The Router must parse location.hash by splitting on the first `?` character: `const [path, qs] = location.hash.slice(1).split('?', 2); const query = Object.fromEntries(new URLSearchParams(qs || ''));` — include this exact parsing logic in the spec to prevent misimplementation.

objections.description: `AbortController.timeout(8000)` is a static method introduced in Node 17.3+ and available in browsers only since Chrome 103 / Safari 16 / Firefox 100. Your browser support floor is Safari 15 and Chrome 89. This API does not exist on those browsers. The timeout will silently not work — no abort, no error, requests hang forever on slow connections.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace AbortController.timeout() with a manual timeout pattern: create a standard AbortController, start a setTimeout(8000) that calls controller.abort(), and clear the timeout on response/error. This works on every browser in the support matrix. Document in useFetch implementation notes.

objections.description: StatusCard polls every 30 seconds with aria-live='polite', but the spec doesn't define what happens when the component unmounts mid-poll. If the poll's setInterval fires after unmount, it'll attempt a state update on an unmounted component. Additionally, if the user switches tabs (document.hidden), polling should pause — burning API calls and battery while the tab is backgrounded is unacceptable on mobile.
objections.suggested_fix: Add to Section 6 or Section 4: 'StatusCard polling uses setInterval inside useEffect with cleanup. Polling MUST pause when document.visibilityState === "hidden" via a visibilitychange listener. On visibility restore, fire an immediate fetch then resume interval. The useEffect cleanup function clears both the interval and the visibility listener.'

objections.description: The esbuild command uses `--target=es2020` but the browser support floor includes Safari 15 which shipped Dec 2021. ES2020 features like optional chaining and nullish coalescing are fine on Safari 15, but if any authored code uses ES2022 features (top-level await, Array.at(), Object.hasOwn(), class static blocks), esbuild's es2020 target will NOT downlevel all of them — esbuild doesn't polyfill built-in methods like Array.at(). The target protects syntax but not API surface.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a note under Build System: 'esbuild --target=es2020 downlevels SYNTAX only, not runtime APIs. Do not use Array.prototype.at(), Object.hasOwn(), structuredClone(), or other post-ES2020 built-in methods without checking browser support. Add this as a lint rule or PR checklist item.' Alternatively, set --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 for more precise output.

objections.description: The health check gates ALL screen-specific fetches. If /api/health returns a transient 503 but the rest of the API is functional (common during rolling deploys where health checks fail before routes), the entire app shows a dead screen. A single flaky health endpoint should not be a total availability blocker.
objections.suggested_fix: Change health check behavior to: on failure, show a non-blocking warning banner at the top of Shell ('Server connectivity issue — some features may be unavailable') and proceed to mount screens with their own fetch cycles. Only show the full-screen error state if BOTH health check AND the first screen-specific fetch fail. This provides graceful degradation instead of a binary kill switch.

objections.location: Section 7 — tokens.css / --text-tertiary contrast in dark mode
objections.description: The comment claims --text-tertiary (#71717a / zinc-500) has 4.6:1 contrast on --surface-0 (#09090b). Actual calculated contrast ratio of #71717a on #09090b is approximately 4.57:1 — this technically passes AA for normal text (≥4.5:1) but is razor-thin margin. Any slight color shift from font rendering, subpixel antialiasing, or screen calibration could push it below threshold. More critically, --text-tertiary on --surface-1 (#18181b) drops to ~4.1:1, which fails AA. The spec uses --text-tertiary for tab labels and secondary info — these WILL appear on surface-1 cards.
objections.suggested_fix: Bump dark-mode --text-tertiary to #7e7e88 or #82828c (targeting ≥5.0:1 on surface-0 and ≥4.5:1 on surface-1). Recalculate and document contrast ratios for EVERY surface pairing where tertiary text appears, not just surface-0.

objections.description: Inter font files listed as inter-regular.woff2, inter-medium.woff2, inter-semibold.woff2 but no @font-face declarations are specified anywhere in the artifact. tokens.css references the font-family but doesn't include the @font-face rules that map weight values to files. Without these, the browser will use the system fallback stack and Inter never loads.
objections.suggested_fix: Add @font-face declarations to tokens.css (or a dedicated fonts.css imported first) with explicit font-weight mappings: 400 → inter-regular.woff2, 500 → inter-medium.woff2, 600 → inter-semibold.woff2. Include font-display: swap in each declaration.

suggestions.description: Use esbuild's browser-list target format `--target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90` instead of `--target=es2020`. This is more precise, self-documenting of the actual support matrix, and lets esbuild make optimal decisions per-browser rather than per-spec-version.

The spec says 'WAI-ARIA tabs pattern' but bottom navigation bars are semantically `role=navigation` with links, not `role=tablist` with `role=tab`. The tablist pattern implies content panels switch without page navigation and requires aria-controls pointing to panel IDs. Since these are route-level navigations that change the URL, use `<nav aria-label="Main"> <a role="link">` with aria-current="page" on the active link. This matches WAI-ARIA Practices guidance for site navigation, not tab panels.

Add a `<SkipLink>` component as the very first child of Shell that becomes visible on focus: 'Skip to main content' linking to the main element's ID. This is a baseline WCAG 2.4.1 requirement that's missing entirely.

Add a retry strategy for retryable errors: single automatic retry with 1-second delay before surfacing the error to the user. This eliminates the majority of transient network blips without complex retry logic. The manual 'Retry' button in ErrorState remains as the user-initiated fallback.

Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that sets all --duration-* tokens to 0ms. The spec mentions no reduced-motion accommodation. Without this, users with vestibular disorders get all animations at full duration — a WCAG 2.3.3 failure.

Add Step M3.5: verify that web_server.py Content-Type headers for .js files are `application/javascript` (not `text/plain`). Some simple Python HTTP servers serve .js with wrong MIME type, which causes browsers to refuse module execution with a strict MIME type error. This is a silent, hard-to-diagnose failure.

objections.description: The navigation model conflates linear browser history with hierarchical drill-down semantics. The spec describes tabs as 'parallel peer destinations' yet implements them as hash routes that push history entries. If a user flows Home → Detail (drill-down) → Search (tab switch), the history stack becomes [#/, #/doc/123, #/search]. Pressing Back from Search returns to Detail (a dead end) rather than Home, violating the tab paradigm where switching tabs should replace, not push, state. This creates a 'history trap' where users cannot efficiently switch between primary sections without backtracking through drill-down contexts.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement history.replaceState() for all tab switches (Home/Search/Library), ensuring the browser history reflects only the drill-down hierarchy, not tab context switches. Document explicitly: tabs are persistent UI state, not history entries.

objections.location: Section 3: Drill-down views and Section 4: DetailScreen
objections.description: Deep linking directly to #/doc/:id presents a broken back navigation affordance. The Detail screen displays a chevron-left 'Back' button, but when accessed directly (shared link, refresh), window.history.length === 1 and there is no parent to return to. The spec provides no fallback behavior (e.g., redirect to Home, or swap 'Back' for 'Close'), leaving users stranded in a dead-end view.
objections.suggested_fix: Detect direct entry (history.state === null or history.length === 1) in DetailScreen. When detected, replace the 'Back' button with a 'Home' link (href='#/') and skip the history.back() call. Alternatively, inject a synthetic Home entry into the stack before the detail route on direct entry.

objections.location: Section 6: Data fetching and Section 4: Screen components
objections.description: The 'fetch-on-mount' pattern combined with conditional route rendering (route → Screen component) guarantees state loss on tab switching. When a user scrolls through Search results, switches to Library, then returns to Search, SearchScreen unmounts and remounts, destroying scroll position, input text, and fetched results. This 'pogo-sticking' penalty makes cross-reference workflows (comparing items across tabs) unusable.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement the 'Persistent Tab Panel' pattern: render all three tab screens simultaneously in the DOM, using CSS (display: none or visibility/opacity with inert) to show/hide, rather than conditional rendering. This preserves component state and scroll position. Alternatively, hoist Search state to the Router context and implement scroll restoration via sessionStorage, though this is more fragile.

objections.location: Section 3: Search presets and Section 5: searchDocuments
objections.description: The synchronization between search input state and URL query parameters (?q=) is undefined. If the user types a manual query (not using presets), the URL does not update, making the search non-shareable and vulnerable to refresh data loss. If the implementation updates the URL on input, it risks polluting history with every keystroke (unless using replaceState). The spec mentions presets set the query param but omits the manual typing behavior entirely.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify that SearchScreen uses history.replaceState (not pushState) to sync the ?q= parameter as the user types (debounced at 300ms). This ensures shareability without history pollution. Explicitly ban pushState for input-driven updates.

objections.location: Section 3: Bottom tab bar and Section 7: Design tokens
objections.description: The fixed bottom tab bar lacks safe area handling for iOS Safari (env(safe-area-inset-bottom)), guaranteeing that the tab bar will be obscured by the browser's native bottom bar or cause accidental home bar interactions. Additionally, the spec omits pull-to-refresh behavior, a mandatory mobile interaction pattern for content feeds.
objections.suggested_fix: Add --safe-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) to tokens.css and apply padding-bottom: var(--safe-bottom) to the TabBar container. Specify pull-to-refresh implementation: use overscroll-behavior-y: contain on the main scroll container and implement a pull-to-refresh hook that triggers on negative scroll with resistance.

objections.description: Focus management on sheet close assumes the trigger element remains mounted and visible. If the trigger was inside a virtualized list (Timeline, Results) and the user scrolled, the element may be unmounted from the DOM or recycled. Calling .focus() on a detached element throws or sends focus to body, breaking keyboard navigation flow.
objections.suggested_fix: Before returning focus, check triggerRef.current?.isConnected. If false, fall back to focusing the screen container (document.querySelector('[role=main]')) or the first focusable element in the new visible set.

suggestions.description: Add an 'IA Guard' component that monitors route changes and enforces the 'tabs replace history' rule, logging violations in development. This prevents future developers from accidentally using pushState in tab handlers.

Add semantic motion tokens for 'exit' vs 'enter' durations. Currently only generic durations are defined. Drill-down transitions should be faster (200ms) than sheet entrances (300ms) to reinforce the hierarchy.

Explicitly map the 404 handling behavior for API routes to the ErrorState component variants. Distinguish between 'endpoint not found' (dev error) and 'resource not found' (valid 404), as they require different UX (retry vs 'item deleted').

objections.description: Design token file is truncated mid-declaration at '--skeleton-base: #e4e4e7; /* surface' — spec is incomplete and cannot be implemented. Light mode token definitions are missing entirely.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the light mode token block with all missing values: skeleton-shine, spacing, typography scale, radii, shadows, motion, and tab-bar tokens. Verify file integrity before convergence approval.

objections.description: Contrast ratio claims are inaccurate. text-tertiary (#71717a) on surface-0 (#09090b) calculates to ~4.0:1, not 4.6:1 — fails WCAG AA 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Light mode text-tertiary (#636370) on white is ~4.4:1, not 4.7:1.
objections.suggested_fix: Adjust text-tertiary to #787882 for dark mode (4.6:1 verified) and #585865 for light mode (4.8:1 verified). Update all contrast claims with actual calculated values.

objections.description: No prefers-reduced-motion media query handling specified anywhere in the spec. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A requirement (Success Criterion 2.3.3). Users with vestibular disorders will experience motion sickness from sheet animations and transitions.
objections.suggested_fix: Add to animations.css: '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }'

objections.description: Bottom sheet drag handle (4px tall × 32px wide) violates minimum touch target size. WCAG 2.2 requires 24px minimum, but platform guidelines (iOS HIG, Material) require 44×44px interactive area. Current spec only defines visual dimensions.
objections.suggested_fix: Define drag handle as 44×44px interactive area with 4px×32px visual indicator centered inside. Add '--touch-target-min: 44px' token and enforce via CSS min-height/min-width on all interactive elements.

objections.description: Status colors rely exclusively on hue differentiation (success/warning/error/info/pending). Colorblind users (8% of males) cannot distinguish red/green status states without additional indicators.
objections.suggested_fix: Add non-color status indicators: '--status-success-shape: circle', '--status-warning-shape: triangle', '--status-error-shape: square' OR mandate icon + color pairing in component specs. Document in accessibility section.

objections.description: Focus ring specification '--shadow-focus: 0 0 0 2px var(--surface-0), 0 0 0 4px var(--accent-default)' will disappear in light mode when surface-0 (#ffffff) matches page background. The 2px gap becomes invisible.
objections.suggested_fix: Use '--shadow-focus: 0 0 0 2px var(--surface-1), 0 0 0 4px var(--accent-default)' where surface-1 provides contrast in both themes, or define separate focus tokens per theme mode.

objections.description: Tab bar active indicator (3px bottom border) is insufficient for low-vision users. The 3px line may not be perceivable at typical viewing distances, especially on lower-PPI displays.
objections.suggested_fix: Increase to 4px minimum, add secondary indicator (icon fill state change is present but should be documented as required, not optional), and ensure active tab has both color AND weight differentiation.

objections.description: Icon stroke width (1.5px) is fixed but icon sizes vary (16/20/24px). A 1.5px stroke on 16px icons appears heavier than on 24px icons, creating visual inconsistency across the interface.
objections.suggested_fix: Define stroke width as percentage or provide size-specific stroke tokens: '--stroke-xs: 1.25px' for 16px icons, '--stroke-sm: 1.5px' for 20px, '--stroke-md: 1.75px' for 24px.

suggestions.description: Add CSS custom property for high-contrast mode detection: '--user-contrast-preference: 0' with media query override. Enables future high-contrast theme without redesign.
Add semantic color aliases: '--color-danger' = '--status-error', '--color-caution' = '--status-warning' for clearer intent in component code.
Add '--will-change-sheet: transform' token for bottom sheet GPU acceleration, with note to apply sparingly to avoid memory overhead.

objections.description: AbortController.timeout() is NOT supported in Safari 15 (your stated browser floor). It was added in Safari 16. This will throw a TypeError at runtime in your minimum supported Safari version.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace with manual timeout pattern: const controller = new AbortController(); const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 8000); fetch(url, { signal: controller.signal }).finally(() => clearTimeout(timeoutId));

objections.description: Spec requires 'mobile-first responsive 375→768→1440' but tokens.css contains ZERO breakpoint custom properties or media query definitions. No --breakpoint-sm/md/lg tokens, no responsive utility classes documented. This is a fundamental gap.
objections.suggested_fix: Add breakpoint tokens: --bp-sm: 640px; --bp-md: 768px; --bp-lg: 1024px; --bp-xl: 1440px; Document the media query mixin pattern or Tailwind @apply strategy for responsive utilities.

objections.description: Spec mandates React.memo on list items but objects with identical shape (Result, TimelineEntry) will trigger re-renders because shallow comparison fails on nested properties. Without custom comparison functions, memo is theatrical.
objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) use useMemo on the parent's list array to maintain referential equality, or (2) provide comparison functions: React.memo(ResultCard, (prev, next) => prev.result.id === next.result.id && prev.result.timestamp === next.result.timestamp)

objections.location: ARTIFACT TRUNCATED — tokens.css cuts off mid-token
objections.description: The artifact ends at '--skeleton-base: #e4e4e7; /* surface' — incomplete. Cannot approve a spec that is literally cut off. Missing: complete light mode tokens, animation keyframes, motion utility classes, z-index application, focus states.
objections.suggested_fix: Resubmit complete artifact with all tokens.css content, animations.css definitions, and any remaining sections.

objections.location: Section 5 — API health check blocks entire app
objections.description: If fetchHealthCheck() fails on Shell mount, the entire app shows a full-screen error. This creates a single point of failure and poor UX for transient network issues. A single failed health check should not prevent cached/optimistic UI from rendering.
objections.suggested_fix: Health check should be non-blocking. Show the shell with cached content (if any), display a subtle offline indicator, and retry health check with exponential backoff. Only show full-screen error after 3 consecutive failures.

objections.location: Section 6 — Missing virtualization for 30-item paginated lists
objections.description: 30 items on mobile (375px) with rich card components will cause layout thrashing and memory pressure. React reconciliation of 30 complex list items on each data fetch is expensive without virtualization.
objections.suggested_fix: Document v1 trade-off explicitly. If >20 items render on mobile, add a performance budget per list (e.g., max 15 visible DOM nodes, use CSS content-visibility: auto for off-screen items). Plan for react-window in v2.

objections.location: Section 3 — No scroll restoration on back navigation
objections.description: Hash-based routing with History API navigation stack has no scroll restoration strategy documented. User drills into detail from search results, scrolls, hits back — they lose scroll position. This is a core UX regression.
objections.suggested_fix: Add scroll restoration to navigation.jsx: store scrollY in session state keyed by route on PUSH, restore on BACK. Or use CSS scroll-snap on list containers to make restoration less critical.

objections.location: Section 1 — No Core Web Vitals targets
objections.description: Bundle budget exists but no LCP, FID, or CLS targets defined. A 60KB gzipped bundle can still produce terrible LCP if critical rendering path is blocked. Performance budget is incomplete without these.
objections.suggested_fix: Add targets: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1. Document that index.html must inline critical CSS for above-fold content, defer non-critical CSS, and that fonts must use font-display: swap to prevent FOIT.

suggestions.description: Add a requestIdleCallback polyfill or wrapper for non-critical work (analytics, prefetching) to avoid blocking main thread during interactions.
The spec correctly adds inert to tab bar, but should also add aria-hidden='true' to the main content area behind the sheet to prevent screen readers from escaping the trap via landmarks.

Document the exact TypeScript-like signature: useFetch<T>(fetchFn: (signal: AbortSignal) => Promise<T>, deps: any[]): { data: T | null, loading: boolean, error: ApiError | null, retry: () => void }. Type safety prevents runtime errors from API contract drift.

orchestrator_response.content:
Production Frontend Implementation Spec — v4 (Convergence Round 4)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
0. PRE-MIGRATION AUDIT & BACKEND ENDPOINT MAPPING
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Before any frontend code is written, execute this checklist:

Step 1: Audit web_server.py routes.
  - List every route handler in web_server.py.
  - Map each existing endpoint to the api.js fetch function it will serve.
  - If backend uses /api/v1/search, api.js calls /api/v1/search — the frontend adapts to the backend, never the reverse.
  - Document the exact mapping in a ROUTES.md file at repo root.

Step 2: Confirm static-file serving behavior.
  - Verify web_server.py serves index.html for GET / with no path-based routing for sub-pages.
  - Confirm it serves static assets from src/frontend/web/ (CSS, JS, fonts, images).
  - If web_server.py has catch-all or path-based HTML serving, document it — this affects routing strategy.

Step 3: Verify API health endpoint exists.
  - If GET /api/health (or equivalent) exists, map it. If not, flag for backend team to add.
  - api.js uses this on Shell mount to display connectivity warnings (non-blocking — see Section 5).

Step 4: Runtime 404 detection — distinguish dev errors from data 404s.
  - api.js must log a clear console.error with the endpoint path when it receives a 404.
  - API-route 404 message: "Endpoint not found: GET /api/foo — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md"
  - Resource 404 (e.g., /api/docs/:id where :id doesn't exist): render <ErrorState type="not-found" message="This item may have been deleted" /> — no retry button.
  - Dev-error 404 (endpoint itself missing): render <ErrorState type="endpoint-missing" message="Feature unavailable" onRetry /> — retryable.
  - Distinguish by convention: if the route pattern matches a known endpoint template but the specific resource ID fails, it's a resource 404. If the route pattern itself is unknown, it's a dev error.

Step 5: Verify Content-Type headers.
  - Confirm web_server.py serves .js files with Content-Type: application/javascript (not text/plain).
  - Confirm .css files with Content-Type: text/css.
  - Confirm .woff2 files with Content-Type: font/woff2.
  - Some Python HTTP servers (http.server, SimpleHTTPServer) serve incorrect MIME types.
    Browsers refuse ES module execution with strict MIME type errors — this is a silent, hard-to-diagnose failure.
  - If MIME types are wrong, fix in web_server.py before proceeding.

Migration sequence (big-bang replacement — no feature flags):
  Step M1: Copy current index.html → _legacy/index-alpine.html (preservation, not coexistence).
  Step M2: Create new index.html with React mount point, theme script, CSS links.
  Step M3: Build React app → dist/. web_server.py serves new index.html at / unchanged.
  Step M3.5: Verify MIME types (Step 5 above) by loading dist/main.js in browser — check DevTools Network tab for Content-Type and console for MIME errors.
  Step M4: Smoke-test all API routes from new frontend against running backend.
  Step M5: Remove _legacy/ after 2 weeks of production stability.
  Rollback: revert index.html to _legacy/index-alpine.html. Zero backend changes required.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. BUILD SYSTEM & MODULE STRATEGY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Build tool: esbuild (single dependency, zero-config).

Decision: esbuild bundles React into the output. No vendor/ directory. No import maps.
Rationale: import maps require Safari 16.4+ / Firefox 108+ minimum. Bundling React eliminates
the compatibility risk entirely and simplifies the build to a single command. The bundle size
difference is zero since React ends up in the output either way.

Command:
  esbuild src/frontend/web/src/main.jsx \
    --bundle \
    --format=esm \
    --outdir=src/frontend/web/dist \
    --minify \
    --target=safari15,chrome89,firefox90 \
    --metafile=meta.json

Target rationale: using explicit browser targets instead of es2020 lets esbuild make
optimal per-browser syntax decisions. This is self-documenting of the actual support matrix.

IMPORTANT: esbuild --target downlevels SYNTAX only, not runtime APIs.
Banned APIs (not available on browser floor):
  - AbortController.timeout() — Safari 16+ only
  - Array.prototype.at() — Safari 15.4+ only (borderline, avoid)
  - Object.hasOwn() — Safari 15.4+ only
  - structuredClone() — Safari 15.4+ only
  - crypto.randomUUID() — Safari 15.4+ only
  Add this as a lint rule or PR checklist item. Use Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call() etc.

No --splitting in v1 (splitting requires import maps for shared chunks in browsers;
we just eliminated import maps). Single entry bundle. Code-split in v2 via dynamic import()
if bundle exceeds budget.

Bundle budget enforcement:
  CI step parses meta.json, fails build if dist/main.js gzipped > 80KB.
  Budget breakdown (estimated):
    React 18 + ReactDOM:  ~42KB gzipped
    App components:       ~15KB gzipped
    Icons (tree-shaken):  ~3KB gzipped
    CSS (tokens + anim):  ~4KB gzipped (loaded separately, not in JS budget)
    Total JS:             ~60KB gzipped (20KB headroom to 80KB cap)

Preact escape hatch: if bundle exceeds 80KB, switch via esbuild alias:
  --alias:react=preact/compat --alias:react-dom=preact/compat
  Preact compat is ~4KB vs React's 42KB. Document this as v1.1 option, not default.

Core Web Vitals targets (non-negotiable):
  LCP: < 2.5s on 3G throttled
  FID / INP: < 100ms
  CLS: < 0.1
  Implementation requirements to hit these:
    - index.html inlines critical CSS for above-fold content (shell chrome, skeleton placeholders).
    - tokens.css and animations.css loaded via <link rel="stylesheet"> (not @import — avoids waterfall).
    - Fonts loaded via <link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" crossorigin> with font-display: swap.
    - All above-fold layout uses explicit dimensions (no layout shift from late-loading content).
    - Skeleton placeholders match final content dimensions to prevent CLS on data arrival.

Source code lives in src/frontend/web/src/ (JSX, full React idioms).
Built output lands in src/frontend/web/dist/ (static ES module, minified, tree-shaken).
index.html references dist/main.js via <script type="module">.
web_server.py serves src/frontend/web/ unchanged — only static file additions.

Browser support floor: Chrome 89+, Safari 15+, Firefox 90+, Edge 89+.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2. FILE LAYOUT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

src/frontend/web/
├── index.html                          # Shell HTML, theme-init script, CSS links, inline critical CSS
├── tokens.css                          # Design tokens (dark default + light override) + @font-face
├── animations.css                      # Keyframes + motion utility classes + reduced-motion overrides
├── fonts/
│   ├── inter-regular.woff2
│   ├── inter-medium.woff2
│   └── inter-semibold.woff2
├── src/                                # SOURCE (JSX, authored code)
│   ├── main.jsx                        # Entry: mounts <App/>
│   ├── app.jsx                         # App root: ThemeProvider → Router → Shell
│   ├── state/
│   │   ├── theme.jsx                   # ThemeContext + useTheme hook
│   │   ├── navigation.jsx              # RouterContext + useRoute, hash-based routing
│   │   └── use-fetch.js               # useFetch hook: fetch-on-mount + manual AbortController timeout
│   ├── api.js                          # All fetch functions, manual timeout, error handling
│   ├── utils.js                        # cx() class merger, debounce, format helpers, idle-callback wrapper
│   ├── icons/                          # One file per icon, Phosphor-style, size-specific stroke widths
│   │   ├── home.jsx
│   │   ├── search.jsx
│   │   ├── library.jsx
│   │   ├── chevron-left.jsx
│   │   ├── more-horizontal.jsx
│   │   ├── status-dot.jsx              # Includes shape variants: circle/triangle/square for colorblind support
│   │   ├── alert-circle.jsx
│   │   └── index.js                    # Barrel re-export (tree-shaken by esbuild)
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── shell.jsx                   # Main layout: skip-link + header + content + tab bar
│   │   ├── skip-link.jsx               # WCAG 2.4.1 skip navigation link
│   │   ├── tab-bar.jsx                 # Bottom nav (role=navigation, aria-current=page)
│   │   ├── connectivity-banner.jsx     # Non-blocking health check warning banner
│   │   ├── bottom-sheet.jsx            # Draggable sheet w/ focus trap + portal
│   │   ├── focus-trap.jsx              # Generic focus trap utility
│   │   ├── portal.jsx                  # React portal to document.body
│   │   ├── skeleton.jsx                # High-fidelity skeleton variants with fixed dimensions
│   │   ├── status-card.jsx             # Live operational status with visibility-aware polling
│   │   ├── kpi-metric.jsx              # Single KPI display (React.memo with comparator)
│   │   ├── timeline-item.jsx           # Single timeline row (React.memo with comparator)
│   │   ├── result-card.jsx             # Single search result (React.memo with comparator)
│   │   ├── history-entry.jsx           # Single history row (React.memo with comparator)
│   │   ├── search-composer.jsx         # Search input + preset chips
│   │   ├── empty-state.jsx             # Empty/zero-data illustration
│   │   ├── error-state.jsx             # API failure w/ retry action — supports type variants
│   │   ├── error-boundary.jsx          # React error boundary (catches render crashes)
│   │   └── route-announcer.jsx         # aria-live region for SPA navigation
│   └── screens/
│       ├── home.jsx                    # Dashboard / Home tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       ├── search.jsx                  # Search tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       ├── library.jsx                 # Library/History tab (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
│       └── detail.jsx                  # Drill-down timeline view (wrapped in ErrorBoundary)
├── dist/                               # BUILD OUTPUT (gitignored)
│   └── main.js
└── _legacy/                            # Quarantined old Alpine files (removed after 2 weeks)
    └── index-alpine.html

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3. NAVIGATION & INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Bottom tab bar: 3 tabs (Home, Search, Library).
  - Tabs are parallel peer destinations. No drill-down views in tabs.
  - Semantic markup: <nav aria-label="Main"> with <a> elements, NOT role="tablist".
    Rationale: these are route-level navigations that change the URL, not panel switches.
    Active link gets aria-current="page". Inactive links have no aria-current.
  - Active tab visual: accent-colored filled icon + label text + 4px bottom border (--accent-default).
    Inactive: outline-stroke icon + --text-tertiary label. Both color AND weight differentiation required.
  - Tab bar receives `inert` attribute + `aria-hidden="true"` when bottom sheet is open.
    CSS: `[inert] { visibility: hidden; }` (prevents screen reader and keyboard access).
    Main content area ALSO receives `inert` + `aria-hidden="true"` when bottom sheet is open.
    Focus returns to the trigger element that opened the sheet on close.

  Tab switch history behavior: REPLACE, not PUSH.
    Tab switches use history.replaceState() — they do NOT create history entries.
    Only drill-down navigations (Home→Detail, Search→Detail) use history.pushState().
    This prevents the "history trap": Home → Detail → Search (replace) → Back returns to Detail → Back returns to Home.
    Correct behavior: Back from a tab always returns to the previous drill-down context, not to another tab.

  iOS safe area: Tab bar applies padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px).
    index.html includes <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover">.
    --safe-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) defined in tokens.css.

Drill-down views (Detail/Timeline): accessed from tapping items on Home or Search results.
  - Back affordance: chevron-left button in top-left header area (44×44px tap target).
  - Push onto history via pushState (not replaceState).
  - Deep link handling: when DetailScreen mounts and detects direct entry (no history.state
    or the router's internal stack is empty), the back button renders as a Home link (href="#/")
    instead of calling history.back(). This prevents stranding users at a dead-end.

Routing strategy: hash-based (#/) for zero-config static serving.
  Confirmed: web_server.py is a static file server. It does NOT serve route-specific HTML.
  Hash routing is an intentional v1 decision.
  v2 escape hatch: migrate to History API pushState + web_server.py catch-all that serves
  index.html for all non-API, non-static paths. Document in ARCHITECTURE.md.

  CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION NOTE — Hash query param parsing:
    Hash query params are NOT accessible via location.search. The browser sees everything
    after # as the fragment. The Router MUST parse location.hash manually:

      const raw = location.hash.slice(1) || '/';
      const [path, qs] = raw.split('?', 2);
      const query = Object.fromEntries(new URLSearchParams(qs || ''));

    If someone implements useRoute() by reading new URL(location).searchParams, every
    preset-initiated search will silently fail with an empty query. This parsing logic
    is the ONLY correct approach for hash-based routing with query params.

  Routes:
    #/              → Home screen
    #/search        → Search screen (reads ?q= param for preset-initiated searches)
    #/library       → Library screen
    #/doc/:id       → Detail/Timeline screen (drill-down, not a tab)

  Base path variable: const BASE = '' (empty string). If app moves to /app/ subpath,
  change to '/app' and all route matching prepends BASE. Defined once in navigation.jsx.

  state/navigation.jsx exports:
    RouterContext: { path, params, query, push(path), replace(path), back(), isDirectEntry }
    useRoute(): reads context
    <Router>: listens to hashchange, parses hash path + query params (using above logic),
      updates context, announces to route-announcer, manages scroll restoration

  Focus management on route change:
    - Each screen's root container has tabindex="-1" (enables programmatic focus).
    - On route change, focus moves to the screen container element.
    - :focus-visible styles apply; :focus:not(:focus-visible) gets outline: none (no ugly ring on mouse click).
    - RouteAnnouncer speaks "Navigated to {page name}" via aria-live="assertive".

  Scroll restoration:
    - On pushState (drill-down): store window.scrollY in a Map keyed by the outgoing route path.
    - On back navigation: restore scrollY from the Map after the target screen renders (requestAnimationFrame).
    - On replaceState (tab switch): scroll to 0 (tabs are fresh contexts, but see Persistent Tabs below).
    - sessionStorage is NOT used — the Map lives in memory. Scroll positions are transient.

Search presets on Home: tapping a preset navigates to #/search?q={encodeURIComponent(preset.query)}.
  SearchScreen reads ?q= from the hash query on mount and auto-executes the search.
  The Search tab becomes visually active. The preset is a shortcut, not a duplicate search surface.

  Manual search URL sync: SearchScreen uses history.replaceState (never pushState) to update
  #/search?q={value} as the user types, debounced at 300ms. This ensures:
    - Search is shareable/bookmarkable at any point.
    - Refresh preserves the current query.
    - History is NOT polluted with per-keystroke entries.

Persistent Tab Panels (state preservation on tab switch):
  All three tab screens (Home, Search, Library) render simultaneously in the DOM.
  Only the active tab's container is visible; inactive tabs use:
    CSS: display: none;
    Attribute: inert (prevents keyboard/screen reader interaction with hidden tabs).
  This preserves:
    - Component state (search input text, fetched results, scroll position).
    - Scroll position (each tab's scroll container retains its scrollTop).
    - Avoids re-fetch on every tab switch.
  The Detail screen (#/doc/:id) is NOT persistent — it mounts/unmounts on navigation.
  This is the "render all tabs, show one" pattern. Memory cost is bounded (3 screens, not unbounded).

Bottom sheet:
  - Invoked from contextual buttons (not a tab destination).
  - Opens over content with overlay (--surface-0 at 60% opacity).
  - Drag handle: 44×44px interactive area containing a 32px wide × 4px tall visual indicator,
    --surface-3 background, --radius-full, centered with var(--space-4) top margin.
    The 44×44px area is the touch target; the 4×32px bar is the visual affordance.
  - Dismiss: drag down past 20% of sheet height OR downward velocity > 0.5px/ms, Escape key, overlay tap.
  - Focus trapped inside while open (FocusTrap component).
  - Both TabBar AND <main> receive `inert` + `aria-hidden="true"` when sheet is open.
  - On close: check triggerRef.current?.isConnected before returning focus.
    If trigger is disconnected (scrolled out of virtualized list, unmounted), fall back to
    document.querySelector('[role="main"]')?.focus() or the first focusable element in the viewport.

Quick actions on list items: explicit "⋯" (more) icon button (44×44px tap target). No long-press. No swipe gestures in v1.

Pull-to-refresh: deferred to v2. v1 uses manual refresh via error-state retry buttons and
status card's automatic polling. overscroll-behavior-y: contain applied to main scroll container
to prevent browser default pull-to-refresh (which triggers full page reload and is worse than no PTR).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
4. COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE MAP
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

<App>
├── <ThemeProvider value={{ mode, toggle }}>        // useMemo on value
│   ├── <Router>
│   │   ├── <RouteAnnouncer />                     // aria-live="assertive", sr-only
│   │   ├── <ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorState type="crash" />}>   // last-resort app-level
│   │   │   ├── <Shell>
│   │   │   │   ├── <SkipLink target="#main-content" />  // WCAG 2.4.1 — first focusable element
│   │   │   │   ├── <ConnectivityBanner />               // Non-blocking health warning
│   │   │   │   ├── <header>                             // Sticky top bar, title + optional back/home button
│   │   │   │   ├── <main id="main-content" role="main"> // Screen content
│   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   │   // ─── PERSISTENT TAB PANELS (all mounted, one visible) ───
│   │   │   │   │   ├── <div class={activeTab === 'home' ? '' : 'hidden'} inert={activeTab !== 'home'}>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorState type="crash" />}>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <HomeScreen tabindex="-1">
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <KPIRail>
│   │   │   │   │   │   │   └── <KPIMetric /> ×3 max       // React.memo with comparator
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <StatusCard />                  // aria-live="polite", visibility-aware 30s polling
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <SearchPresets onSelect={→ navigate to #/search?q=} />
│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <RecentResults /> | <EmptyState /> | <ErrorState onRetry />
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   │   ├── <div class={activeTab === 'search' ? '' : 'hidden'} inert={activeTab !== 'search'}>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <SearchScreen tabindex="-1">
│   │   │   │   │   │   ├── <SearchComposer />              // useCallback for debounced handler
│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <ResultsList>                   // paginated, 20-item pages
│   │   │   │   │   │       └── <ResultCard /> ×N           // React.memo with comparator
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ResultsList> | <Skeleton /> | <ErrorState />
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   │   ├── <div class={activeTab === 'library' ? '' : 'hidden'} inert={activeTab !== 'library'}>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │   <LibraryScreen tabindex="-1">
│   │   │   │   │   │   └── <HistoryList>
│   │   │   │   │   │       └── <HistoryEntry /> ×N         // React.memo with comparator
│   │   │   │   │   │   </HistoryList> | <EmptyState />
│   │   │   │   │   │   </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   │   // ─── DRILL-DOWN (conditional, mounts/unmounts) ───
│   │   │   │   │   └── route #/doc/:id →
│   │   │   │   │       <ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │       <DetailScreen tabindex="-1" id={params.id}>
│   │   │   │   │       ├── <StickyHeader title status backOrHome={isDirectEntry ? 'home' : 'back'} />
│   │   │   │   │       ├── <Timeline>                      // paginated, 20-item pages
│   │   │   │   │       │   └── <TimelineItem /> ×N         // React.memo with comparator
│   │   │   │   │       └── <QuickActions onAction={→ opens BottomSheet} />
│   │   │   │   │       </ErrorBoundary>
│   │   │   │   │
│   │   │   │   └── <TabBar />                              // <nav aria-label="Main">, inert when sheet open
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   └── <Portal>
│   │   │       └── <BottomSheet open onClose title>
│   │   │           └── <FocusTrap> {children} </FocusTrap>
│   │   │       </BottomSheet>
│   │   │   </Portal>

ErrorBoundary behavior:
  - Catches React RENDER crashes only. API errors flow through component state → <ErrorState />.
  - Per-screen boundaries isolate failures: a crash in SearchScreen does not take down Home.
  - App-level boundary is the last resort — shows full-screen crash state with "Reload" button.

ErrorState variants:
  - type="crash": render error occurred, "Reload" button (calls location.reload())
  - type="network": API failure, retryable — "Try Again" button
  - type="not-found": resource 404 — "Go Home" link, no retry
  - type="endpoint-missing": dev error — "Try Again" button + console.error guidance
  - type="offline": no connectivity — "Try Again" button + connectivity banner

List performance (v1 trade-off, documented):
  - v1 renders up to 20 items per page. 20 is the max per-page count, not 30 (reduced from v3).
  - Apply content-visibility: auto on list item containers for off-screen paint optimization.
  - v2: if profiling shows >16ms frame times on list render, add react-window virtualization.

// api.js — all functions accept AbortSignal, return typed promises, throw on non-2xx

type Metric = { id: string, label: string, value: string, trend: "up"|"down"|"flat", delta?: string }
type Status = { state: "operational"|"degraded"|"down", message: string, lastUpdated: string /*ISO*/ }
type Preset = { id: string, label: string, query: string, icon?: string }
type Result = { id: string, title: string, snippet: string, timestamp: string, status: string }
type HistoryEntry = { id: string, query: string, resultCount: number, timestamp: string }
type TimelineEntry = { id: string, timestamp: string, label: string, state: "success"|"warning"|"error"|"info"|"pending", detail?: string }
type Action = { id: string, label: string, icon: string, destructive?: boolean }

// Endpoint mapping — ACTUAL paths determined by Section 0 audit.
// These are DEFAULTS; api.js reads from a config object that the audit populates.
fetchHealthCheck(signal?): Promise<{ok: boolean}>        // GET /api/health
fetchMetrics(signal?): Promise<Metric[]>                 // GET /api/metrics
fetchStatus(signal?): Promise<Status>                    // GET /api/status
fetchPresets(signal?): Promise<Preset[]>                 // GET /api/presets
searchDocuments(query, signal?): Promise<Result[]>       // GET /api/search?q=
fetchHistory(signal?): Promise<HistoryEntry[]>           // GET /api/history
fetchTimeline(docId, signal?): Promise<TimelineEntry[]>  // GET /api/docs/:id/timeline
fetchActions(docId, signal?): Promise<Action[]>          // GET /api/docs/:id/actions
executeAction(docId, actionId, signal?): Promise<void>   // POST /api/docs/:id/actions/:actionId

Timeout implementation (browser-floor safe):
  DO NOT use AbortController.timeout() — unavailable on Safari 15 / Chrome 89.
  Manual timeout pattern used in every fetch function:

    async function fetchWithTimeout(url, options = {}, timeoutMs = 8000) {
      const controller = new AbortController();
      const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
      try {
        const response = await fetch(url, { ...options, signal: controller.signal });
        return response;
      } finally {
        clearTimeout(timeoutId);
      }
    }

  If the caller passes its own signal (for cancellation), link it:
    caller's signal.addEventListener('abort', () => controller.abort());
  This ensures both timeout AND caller-initiated cancellation work.

Error handling:
  class ApiError extends Error {
    constructor(status, message, retryable, endpoint) {
      super(message);
      this.status = status;
      this.retryable = retryable;
      this.endpoint = endpoint;
    }
  }
  - All fetchers throw ApiError({ status, message, retryable, endpoint }) on failure.
  - On 404: console.error(`Endpoint not found: ${method} ${url} — verify backend route mapping in ROUTES.md`)
  - AbortError handling: if error.name === 'AbortError', the caller returns silently without updating state.
    This is critical for SearchComposer — cancelled requests must not trigger error states.
  - Callers catch and render <ErrorState /> with appropriate type variant.

Automatic retry for retryable errors:
  - On retryable failure (5xx, timeout), api.js performs ONE automatic retry after 1000ms delay.
  - If retry also fails, throw the error to the caller for UI handling.
  - The manual "Retry" button in ErrorState triggers a fresh attempt (not counted against auto-retry).
  - Non-retryable errors (4xx except 408/429) are thrown immediately, no auto-retry.
  - 429 Too Many Requests: parse Retry-After header, delay accordingly, then retry once.

Health check on Shell mount (NON-BLOCKING):
  - Shell calls fetchHealthCheck() on mount.
  - If health check succeeds: no banner, proceed normally.
  - If health check fails: show <ConnectivityBanner> at top of shell with message
    "Server connectivity issue — some features may be unavailable." Banner is dismissible.
    Screens still mount and attempt their own fetches.
  - If health check fails AND the first screen-specific fetch also fails: show full-screen
    <ErrorState type="offline"> with retry. This is the "both failed" threshold.
  - ConnectivityBanner retries health check every 15 seconds. On success, auto-dismisses.

Autocomplete debounce: 200ms in SearchComposer. Previous in-flight request cancelled via AbortController before new request fires. AbortError is silently swallowed (see above).
